articles.title: South Shore mother and son stuck in Qatar amid war in Iran
articles.description:
“They have reported to us that they’ve been hearing missile launches and perhaps drone explosions, things like that, which is deeply unsettling,” U.S. Rep. Stephen Lynch said.

The post South Shore mother and son stuck in Qatar amid war in Iran appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: South Shore mother and son are stranded in Qatar due to the Iran conflict.
U.S. Rep. Stephen Lynch reported missile launches and drone explosions in the area.
Local families are concerned for the safety of loved ones abroad amid rising tensions.

articles.title: Tufts announces new proposed aquatics center for swimming and diving teams
articles.description:
The university says the aquatics center would replace the roughly 80-year-old Hamilton Pool, which currently cannot host conference championship meets, requiring the teams to travel for some practices and competitions.

The post Tufts announces new proposed aquatics center for swimming and diving teams appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Tufts University proposes a new aquatics center to replace the 80-year-old Hamilton Pool.
The new facility aims to host conference championship meets for swimming and diving teams.
Local athletes will benefit from reduced travel for practices and competitions.

articles.title: Circle Furniture to auction off remaining inventory after bankruptcy
articles.description:
The longtime Massachusetts furniture retailer, which abruptly closed in December, is selling off hundreds of showroom and warehouse items.

The post Circle Furniture to auction off remaining inventory after bankruptcy appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Circle Furniture, a Massachusetts retailer, closed in December and is auctioning remaining inventory.
Hundreds of showroom and warehouse items will be available for auction starting March 2026.
Local residents can purchase discounted furniture as the auction aims to clear out remaining stock.

articles.title: Watch the N.H. Liquor & Wine Outlet video that’s lowkey going viral
articles.description:
"The response has been mind blowing," says Mark Roy, the video's star and the outlet's director of marketing.

The post Watch the N.H. Liquor & Wine Outlet video that’s lowkey going viral appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Mark Roy, director of marketing, stars in a viral video promoting N.H. Liquor & Wine Outlet.
The video has garnered significant attention, indicating strong community engagement with local businesses.
Boston residents may benefit from increased awareness and potential promotions at nearby liquor outlets.

articles.title: Bruins trade deadline live updates: Will Don Sweeney add or subtract from Boston’s roster?
articles.description:
It remains unclear if the Bruins will decide to buy or sell before Friday's deadline.

The post Bruins trade deadline live updates: Will Don Sweeney add or subtract from Boston’s roster? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bruins' trade deadline decision remains uncertain as of March 6, 2026.
General Manager Don Sweeney faces pressure to either buy or sell players.
Local fans await roster changes that could impact playoff chances this season.

articles.title: Job interviews now include AI and personality tests. Here’s what candidates should know.
articles.description:
From typing tests to AI-powered video screenings, pre-employment assessments are becoming more complex — and in some cases, legally risky.

The post Job interviews now include AI and personality tests. Here’s what candidates should know. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Employers in Boston are increasingly using AI and personality tests in job interviews.
Complex assessments may lead to legal risks for companies hiring in Massachusetts.
Candidates must adapt to new interview formats to remain competitive in the job market.

articles.title: You can be the proud owner of an entire 21-building Maine village. It’s kind of a steal.
articles.description:
A nearly 40-acre, 21-building property in Pittston, Maine — an “antique village” known as Tut Hill — is once again on the market.

The post You can be the proud owner of an entire 21-building Maine village. It’s kind of a steal. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Tut Hill, a 21-building village in Pittston, Maine, is back on the market.
The property spans nearly 40 acres and offers unique investment opportunities.
Boston residents may find affordable vacation or retreat options just a short drive away.
